name: Cornerstone Community
description_short: Cornerstone Community Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving Western New York for 68 years with 6 branches and a 7th opening in 2026.
description_long:
Cornerstone Community Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution that has served Western New York communities for nearly seven decades. Founded on cooperative principles, the credit union operates as a NCUA-insured institution where members are owners rather than customers, giving them voting rights and a stake in institutional decisions. The credit union has grown to serve members across multiple branches in Lockport, Amherst, Middleport, Niagara Falls, and North Tonawanda, with plans to open a seventh location in the City of Lockport in late winter/early spring 2026.

Cornerstone offers a comprehensive suite of financial products typical of credit unions, including consumer checking and savings accounts with competitive rates (Community Checking earning 5.25% APY on balances up to $10,000), home equity lines of credit (3.49% introductory APR for 12 months), mortgage services, auto loans (rates as low as 5.54% APR), personal loans (9.49% APR), share certificates (up to 3.25% APY), and credit cards (7.90% APR). The institution also provides business banking services for commercial members and access to more than 5,000 CO-OP shared branch locations nationwide.

What distinguishes Cornerstone is its emphasis on community-focused banking and member ownership. The marketing messaging centers on transparency, member-first values, and the idea that member deposits stay within the local community. The institution highlights its long tenure in Western New York (68 years) and positions itself against larger banking institutions by emphasizing personalized service and genuine member care rather than "flashy promises." Member testimonials reference empathy and helpfulness that differentiates the credit union from broader banking competitors.

Cornerstone is a legitimate credit union with standard NCUA protections and competitive product offerings. However, prospective members should note that credit union membership typically requires either living/working in specific geographic areas or meeting other membership eligibility criteria (not detailed on the website). The rates advertised are introductory or base rates and actual approval depends on creditworthiness. Like all financial institutions, this is best suited for members who value community banking relationships and member ownership principles over convenience of mega-bank locations.
services: Community Checking with 5.25% APY on balances up to $10,000
Home Equity Lines of Credit (3.49% APR introductory rate for 12 months)
Mortgage services and financing for home purchase/refinance
Business banking services and business member accounts
Loan payment processing and account transfers
Access to 5,000+ CO-OP shared branch network nationwide
Member financial education and advisory services
pros: 5.25% APY on Community Checking up to $10,000 balance—significantly higher than most retail banks
Member-ownership structure means no shareholders to profit from member fees; earnings returned as benefits
Access to 5,000+ CO-OP shared branches nationwide despite having only 6 physical locations
Competitive introductory rate of 3.49% APR for 12 months on home equity lines of credit
Long community presence (68 years serving Western New York) suggests stability and local commitment
Diverse product suite including mortgages, business banking, and share certificates at reasonable rates
Strong member testimonials referencing exceptional service quality and personalized attention
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only 6 current branches all in Western New York; membership eligibility likely restricted
Website does not clearly state membership requirements or eligibility criteria (common barrier for credit unions)
Rates shown are introductory or base rates; actual approval and pricing depend on creditworthiness, not disclosed
No mention of digital banking features, mobile app functionality, or online-only account management capabilities
Newer branch opening in 2026 suggests recent rapid expansion, which may impact service consistency
best_for: Western New York residents/workers seeking member-owned banking with community reinvestment priorities
Savers wanting high-yield checking accounts (5.25% APY on deposits up to $10,000)
Homeowners interested in home equity lines of credit with low introductory rates for refinancing or cash-out scenarios
Small business owners and self-employed individuals needing business banking with a community-focused lender
diagnosis: Cornerstone Community Federal Credit Union is best for Western New York residents who qualify for membership and prioritize community banking, member ownership, and competitive savings rates over convenience of national branch networks. The main caveat is that credit union membership is typically restricted by geography or other eligibility criteria not fully disclosed on the website, and actual rates/approval depend on individual creditworthiness.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Cornerstone Community — NCUA-insured credit union in LOCKPORT, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
